title: Java Access Modifiers
description: Practice class, field, and method visibility.
learningGoals: Recall that public, protected, package-private, and private narrow access in that order.
Recognize that final classes cannot be subclassed.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if another class declares class Sub extends Account?
choices: Sub inherits balance and owner normally
Sub compiles but cannot call deposit
Compilation fails because Account is final and cannot be subclassed
completionChecks.explanation: A final class cannot be extended, so declaring class Sub extends Account fails to compile.

completionChecks.prompt: Which member is visible to subclasses in another package?
choices: None; cross-package access is always denied
completionChecks.explanation: protected members are visible to subclasses even across package boundaries; package-private and private members are not.

completionChecks.prompt: From another class in the same package, what happens when code calls new Account().deposit(10)?
choices: Compilation fails because deposit is private
Compilation fails because Account is final
It compiles; deposit has package-private access and updates the private balance inside Account
completionChecks.explanation: The deposit method has package-private access, so same-package callers can invoke it even though balance remains private.
